Petition For Authority To Enter Into Fee Agreement And To Pay Litigation Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-08-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Martha Daetwyler for Meli Brown (Petitioner)

The Court inquired about the engagement letter. Ms. Daetwyler cited Probate Code 10 553 allowing Administrator to commence and maintain actions, stated the petition was not started until the action was nearing completion, and that fees have not been paid yet. The stated court authorization is required, the agreement is proper, and will approve it.

The Court granted the petition for authority to enter into fee agreement and to pay litigation counsel, (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Continued To Sep-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Martha Daetwyler for and with (petitioner) Meli Brown.

The Court stated that this is a spousal property petition, that the parties intentionally split the holdings into separate property and that the is not sure that there is a legal basis to switch this back to community property. Ms. Daetwyler reported that there is no source of separate property, that in early 2000 the couple had different ideas for the properties and Mr. Brown decided that he would take his, that they recently obtained a property transaction history for the Egbert property, that payments were made from a joint checking account, that Mr. Brown told Mrs. Brown that it would be easier to manage if the property was only in his name, that their argument is that the title on the deed does not indicate the fact of separate property and that the 3 beneficiaries consent. The Court stated that deeds can be a formal transmutation. Ms. Daetwyler stated that she can do additional research and that the additional evidence that they have are checks and a joint checking account. The Court stated that if they maintain separate properties, using community funds to support the properties does not change the way they are held. Ms. Daetwyler stated that the deeds in question were done just for convenience. The Court stated that they could have re-deeded them later, that they cannot disregard that they did do it, that they may wish they had done it differently, that they have a remedy to probate, that it would deny the petition on what it has now but will continue the matter if counsel thinks there is more evidence. Ms. Daetwyler requested a 2 week continuance stating that if she does not think there is anything that will help she will take the matter off calendar. The Petition for Spousal Property was continued to September 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and counsel was instructed to file any additional documents no later than August 30, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
